_meta.description: Portland OR Metro — Manual URL Curation Results for scrape_registry ingestion
_meta.session_context: Step 3 of Portland metro aggressive utility rate scan. All URLs manually verified as containing water rate data. PWSIDs not yet resolved — Claude Code must look them up via SDWIS query before inserting.
_meta.insert_sql_template: INSERT INTO utility.scrape_registry (pwsid, url, url_source, status, notes) VALUES (:pwsid, :url, 'curated_portland', 'pending', 'Portland metro manual curation 2026-03-26') ON CONFLICT (pwsid, url) DO NOTHING;
_meta.pwsid_lookup_sql: SELECT s.pwsid, s.pws_name, s.city, s.state_code, s.population_served_count FROM utility.sdwis_systems s WHERE s.state_code IN ('OR','WA') AND s.pws_type_code = 'CWS' AND (UPPER(s.pws_name) LIKE :name_pattern OR UPPER(s.city) = :city) ORDER BY s.population_served_count DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 5;
_meta.pwsid_resolution_strategy: For each entry, query SDWIS using sdwis_name_hint and sdwis_city fields. If multiple matches, prefer the one with highest population_served_count that matches the state. Confirm match before inserting. Log any unresolvable entries.
coverage_notes: 11 secondary Oregon cities (Salem, Eugene, Springfield, Bend, Medford, Corvallis, Albany, Keizer, Newberg, McMinnville, Scappoose)

utilities.current_rate_summary: FY2025-26: $8.171/ccf volume rate; base charge ~$74.14/bill (varies by billing period length). Rates effective Jul 1, 2025. 8.1% water rate increase.
utilities.pwsid_note: Likely OR4100657 — verify against SDWIS before insert

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates effective Nov 1, 2025. Bimonthly billing. One fixed charge per 2-month cycle. Typical SFR uses 7 CCF/mo (5,236 gal). 3% increase Nov 2025; another 3% Nov 2026.
SFR base: $28.17/mo for 5/8" meter. Bimonthly billing. Volume in units (1 unit = 748 gal/ccf). Five-year rate schedule from 2022; last increase Jan 1, 2025 (+$19.48/bill).
utilities.pwsid_note: Note: Rockwood Water PUD also serves parts of Gresham — this entry is for the City utility only

utilities.current_rate_summary: Flat rate $6.87/unit (748 gal) effective Jul 1, 2025 — 16.5% increase from $5.90. No tiered structure. Monthly meter fee also applies. Willamette Water Supply Program investment driver.
utilities.pwsid_note: Note: TVWD also serves eastern Beaverton (east of Cornelius Pass Rd) — this entry is for City of Beaverton utility only

utilities.current_rate_summary: Fixed charge: $21.94/mo (held flat 2024-2026). New low-use tier (0-4 ccf) at $3.63/ccf introduced 2025. Typical SFR (8 ccf): $53.18/mo in 2025, $56.22 in 2026. 5.7% increase Jan 2025. Rates effective Jan 1.
utilities.pwsid_note: Service area: west of Cornelius Pass Rd only. TVWD serves east Hillsboro. City also serves some unincorporated western Washington County.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Tiered residential rates. Fixed base charge: $30.56/mo (FY2024-25); Utility Fees effective Jul 1, 2025 per Master Fees and Charges. Joint Lake Oswego-Tigard Water Partnership; serves 100,000+ customers between LO and Tigard.
utilities.pwsid_note: Also wholesales to City of Tigard via LO-Tigard Water Partnership

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rate increase effective Jul 1, 2025. Buys treated water from Lake Oswego-Tigard Water Treatment Plant. Monthly billing. Also includes sewer (Clean Water Services), stormwater, street maintenance, parks.
utilities.pwsid_note: Note: Tigard Water District (separate entity) contracts City of Tigard for delivery — PWSID may be under Tigard Water District. Check both in SDWIS.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rate increase effective Sep 1, 2025. Bimonthly billing (cycles 1-3 residential; cycle 4 commercial). Fixed service charge by meter size + commodity charges. Water source: Clackamas River. Two service areas: Clackamas (North) and Clairmont (South).

3-tier rate structure (indoor essential / outdoor essential / additional outdoor). Monthly fixed service charge by meter size. Buys wholesale from Clackamas River Water and North Clackamas County Water Commission. 17,500 connections.
utilities.pwsid_note: Note: dual domains — sunrisewater.com and sunrisewateror.gov both active. Rate page is on sunrisewater.com.

utilities.current_rate_summary: New rate structure effective Jul 1, 2025. Switched from bimonthly to monthly billing Jul 2024. Combined water+wastewater+watershed district. 26.5% increase in 2023 was last major change; 2025 restructure followed rate study.
utilities.pwsid_note: Serves unincorporated area between Gladstone and Milwaukie

utilities.current_rate_summary: SFR rates as of Jul 1, 2024: Water Treatment (South Fork Water Board) $1.7730/CCF + Water Distribution base $20.35 + usage $2.2517/CCF. Jan 1, 2026 increases: Distribution +3%, Treatment +$0.29/unit. Rates by class PDF available.
utilities.pwsid_note: Buys treated water wholesale from South Fork Water Board (jointly owned with West Linn)

utilities.current_rate_summary: Base monthly rate includes first 7 CCF (5,236 gal). Each CCF over 7 is additional charge. Rates adjust Jan 1 annually, up to 5% max without voter approval (City Charter Section 44). Buys treated water from South Fork Water Board.

Water, wastewater (sewer), stormwater, street, and SAFE fees all on one bill. Fee Schedule adopted annually. FY2025 revision (Resolution 42-2024) adopted. Utility fees reviewed by Community Utility Advisory Committee (CUAC) annually.
utilities.pwsid_note: Sewer is via Clackamas County WES Rate Zone 2. Scraper should look for fee schedule PDF linked from billing page.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Water fees last updated Jul 1, 2025 per page notice. Among lowest water rates in metro (~$39.50/mo for 8 ccf per Hillsboro 2024 comparison). Also provides electric service (Forest Grove Light and Power).
utilities.pwsid_note: PWSID confirmed from EWG database entry OR4100305

utilities.current_rate_summary: No water rate increase in 2025 (first increase in 12 years coming Jan 1, 2026). 2026 rate increase: residential +$4-13/mo. New seasonal rate structure: peak Jun-Sep $0.032/cf (2026), off-peak Oct-May $0.025/cf (2026). Serves ~42,000 water customers in unincorporated Clark County plus Ridgefield and Battle Ground.
utilities.pwsid_note: WA utility — check EFC and Duke coverage status before inserting. May already be partially covered.

utilities.current_rate_summary: New tiered water rate billing model adopted Dec 2024, effective 2025. Bimonthly billing for most residential. Volume charged per CCF. Outside city limits: $4.56/CCF (2024 rate). 3rd largest municipal water provider in WA state. 270,000 customers, 77,000 service connections.
utilities.pwsid_note: WA utility. Check EFC/Duke coverage before inserting.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Inside city: 5/8" meter base $14.35 + $2.76/ccf. Outside city: base $21.52 + $4.15/ccf. 6%/yr water rate increases 2024-2028 (adopted Dec 2023). Switched to monthly billing Jun 2025. New customer portal launched 2025.
utilities.pwsid_note: WA utility. Check EFC/Duke coverage before inserting.

utilities.current_rate_summary: New rates effective Jul 15, 2025 (Resolution 2045, Jun 18, 2025). SFR volume rate $6.17/ccf. Water, sewer, and storm on single bill. Also includes $10/mo public safety fee for new police station. Total water revenue budget $4.4M for FY2025-26.

Utility info page confirmed. Monthly billing (15th-to-15th cycle). $150 deposit required. No rate table visible in search results — scraper will need to traverse page for embedded or linked rate data.
utilities.pwsid_note: Rate data NOT confirmed — page exists but rates may be in a linked document. Flag for secondary verification.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Rates effective Oct 1, 2025. SFR example: $16.30 customer charge + $1.7557/unit (first 10 units) + $2.2305/unit (next 5 units) = $45.01 total for typical usage. Combined water+electric utility. Not-for-profit publicly owned.
utilities.pwsid_note: PWSID may be under McMinnville Water & Light or City of McMinnville — check both in SDWIS

utilities.current_rate_summary: 2025 rates (effective Jan 2025, set by Citizens Rate Review Committee biannually): water billing charge $3.79/SFR; water meter charge $21.30/mo (3/4" meter); volume charge $4.59/ccf. Full rate schedule PDF linked from page.

4.5% increase effective Jan 1, 2025; another 4.5% Jan 1, 2026. Typical SFR bill: ~$128/mo (2025). Monthly billing. Water source: Detroit Lake (federally mandated drawdown in 2026 may drive emergency rate increases). Combined water+wastewater+stormwater bill.

Bimonthly billing. Water from 15 groundwater wells (no chlorination needed). FAQ cites $1.77/unit water rate — likely outdated. 3-4% increase discussed for 2025. Sewer operated by City of Salem. Very low rates vs. surrounding cities.
utilities.pwsid_note: Rate data NOT confirmed from current source — $1.77/unit from FAQ may be outdated. Current fee schedule PDF not surfaced. Flag for secondary verification.

utilities.current_rate_summary: 6.6% residential water increase effective Feb 2025. Average water bill: $46/mo (from $43). An additional 8% water increase effective Jan 2026. Combined electric+water utility. Customer-owned. 60,000+ water customers.
utilities.pwsid_note: May be listed as EWEB or Eugene Water & Electric Board in SDWIS

utilities.current_rate_summary: 8% water rate increase 2025; 8.5% effective Jan 1, 2026 (bills Feb 2026). Basic charge: $29.40/mo for 3/4" meter inside city limits. Summer Irrigation Rate in effect Jun-Sep bills. Customer-owned water+electric utility.
Rates effective Jul 1, 2025. Water Services Department. Residential and non-residential rate schedules on page. Winter Quarter Average (Dec-Feb) used for sewer calculation. Monthly billing.

Rates effective Mar 1, 2025: 15.4% increase for SFR 3/4" meter at 12k gal usage (~$5.62/billing). Rates effective Mar 1, 2026: 13.6% increase (SFR avg usage 10k gal: $37.58 → $42.67). Summer/winter seasonal rate structure. Rates reviewed annually in Dec, effective Mar 1. Also serves Central Point, Eagle Point, Jacksonville, Phoenix, Talent, Ashland wholesale.
utilities.pwsid_note: Listed as Medford Water Commission in some sources. Autonomous agency of City of Medford.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Annual review; rates effective Feb 1 each year. Three elevation levels for volume charges (Level 1/2/3) due to pumping requirements. Combined utility bill includes water, wastewater, stormwater, street maintenance, and other city services. Rate table with historical data on page.
Resolution 7415 effective Jul 1, 2025 (most current). All rate resolutions listed on page with PDFs. Rates set by Albany City Council resolution.

utilities.utility_name: City of Medford — Wholesale Customers (Ashland, Central Point, etc.)
utilities.current_rate_summary: Medford Water also publishes wholesale rate schedules for resale customers: Central Point, Eagle Point, Jacksonville, Phoenix, Talent, Ashland. These cities set their own retail rates — this URL covers the wholesale tier only.
utilities.pwsid_note: INFO ONLY — not a direct insert candidate. Each wholesale city (Ashland, Central Point, etc.) has its own PWSID and should be separately curated if needed.

utilities.current_rate_summary: Already listed as id=21 in columbia_yamhill region. Duplicate reference for completeness.
utilities.pwsid_note: Duplicate of id=21 — deduplicate before insert

utilities.current_rate_summary: Sewer rates separate from water. Resolution 2025-09 effective Jul 1, 2025. Water handled by McMinnville Water & Light (see id=20). This URL is for city-administered sewer only.
utilities.pwsid_note: WATER PWSID belongs to McMinnville Water & Light (id=20). This entry is sewer-only — may not belong in scrape_registry if registry is water-only. Include only if schema covers wastewater.

not_found.note: Wholesale-only provider jointly owned by Oregon City and West Linn. No retail customers. Retail rates flow through Oregon City (id=11) and West Linn (id=12). Website: sfwb.com. Do not insert as standalone PWSID.
Small Columbia County city (~2,000 pop). No rate page found within 5-minute search budget. Likely candidate for domain guesser sweep (Step 4).
Serves parts of East Gresham / unincorporated Multnomah County. Not explicitly in the plan's named utility list but appears in Gresham water quality references. May warrant a separate curation.

action_checklist: 1. Run Step 1 SDWIS query to pull all CWS PWSIDs in target counties
2. For each entry in utilities[] where pwsid=null: query SDWIS using sdwis_name_hint and sdwis_city to resolve PWSID
4. For scraper_flag='NEEDS_VERIFICATION': insert URL but flag in notes as needing manual rate data confirmation post-scrape
5. For scraper_flag='SEWER_ONLY_VERIFY_SCHEMA_FIT': check if scrape_registry accepts wastewater URLs before inserting
6. For Clark County WA entries (ids 15-17): check existing EFC/Duke coverage in pwsid_coverage before inserting to avoid duplication
7. Insert all resolved entries using the INSERT SQL template in _meta
9. Separately process WA entries: ua-run-orchestrator --execute 10 --state WA (Clark County only)
